emergent_themes.description: Dramatically different ordering patterns between home and university contexts - frequent at home vs. infrequent but more considered at university
Clear distinction between delivery as solo activity vs. eating out as social activity
Specific concern about service fees as a barrier, separate from delivery fees
Strong mental associations between specific platforms and restaurant types/quality levels

coding_summary: P027 demonstrates a highly context-dependent relationship with delivery, showing restraint at university (fortnightly ordering) but frequent use at home. Her ordering is primarily emotionally triggered (stress relief or celebration) and solo-focused, contrasting with social eating out. She shows strong cost consciousness, particularly around service fees, and has clear platform preferences based on restaurant type rather than brand loyalty. Her approach to subscriptions is purely functional - she won't pay unless usage justifies it. Key insights include the stark home/university behavioral split, the emotional regulation role of delivery, and her pragmatic approach to platform choice.
